description: `let res = state.held;` inside for loop uses block-scoped `let`, shadowing outer `res`. The `res1 = res` on line 148 captures the pre-Stime outer `res`, not the loop result. Loop body could be optimized away.
`let res = state.pending;` inside for loop shadows outer `res`. `res1 = res` on line 163 captures pre-Stime value, not loop result.
`let mode = info.mode;` inside for loop shadows outer `mode`. `res1 = mode` on line 178 captures pre-Stime value, not loop result.
`let contextId = info.contextId;` inside for loop shadows outer `contextId`. `res1 = contextId` on line 193 captures pre-Stime value.
`let available = options.isAvailable;` inside for loop shadows outer `available`. `res = available` on line 208 captures pre-Stime value.
`let signal = options.signal;` inside for loop shadows outer `signal`. `res = signal` on line 223 captures pre-Stime value.
`let timeout = options.timeout;` inside for loop shadows outer `timeout`. `res = timeout` on line 238 captures pre-Stime value.
Uses `let res = result` instead of `let res1 = result`. Naming inconsistent with convention.
Uses `let res = result` instead of `let res1 = result`. Naming inconsistent with convention.
Uses `let res = result` instead of `let res1 = result`.
Uses `let res = result` instead of `let res1 = result`.
Uses `let res = result` instead of `let res1 = result`.
Uses `let res = result` instead of `let res1 = result`.
`let res = AsyncLockMode.EXCLUSIVE;` inside for loop shadows outer `res`. `result = res` on line 58 captures pre-Stime value, not loop result. Loop body could be optimized away.

`let mode = AsyncLockMode.SHARED;` inside loop creates unused local variable (wrong name: `mode` vs outer `res`). Outer `res` is never updated in loop. `result = res` on line 73 captures pre-Stime value. Also double semicolons `;;` on line 73.
`let res = AsyncLockMode.UNLOCKED;` inside for loop shadows outer `res`. `res1 = res` on line 88 captures pre-Stime value, not loop result.
Uses `let res = result` instead of `let res1 = result`.
Uses `let res = result` instead of `let res1 = result`.
Uses `let res = resultA` instead of `let res1 = resultA`.
Uses `let res = resultA` instead of `let res1 = resultA`.
Uses `let res = resultA` instead of `let res1 = resultA`.
Uses `let res = resultA` instead of `let res1 = resultA`.
Uses `let res = resultA` instead of `let res1 = resultA`.
Uses `let res = resultA` instead of `let res1 = resultA`.
For loop contains `rwLock.unlock();` in addition to the tested API `executeReadLock()`. Extra code in the loop may inflate measured time.
For loop contains `await rwLock.executeReadLock();` in addition to the tested API `unlock()`. Extra code in the loop may inflate measured time.
For loop contains `await rLock.lock();` in addition to the tested API `unlock()`. Extra code in the loop may inflate measured time.

Duplicate test case name `PerformanceTest_AtomicFloat_Instance_0200`. Another case at line 391 tests `fetchAdd`, while this one tests `store`. The console.info on line 819 says `0201` but it() name says `0200` — name/log mismatch.
